metadata.title: Model Comparison Surface
metadata.subtitle: Packet fixture for agreement, divergence, and provenance
metadata.description: A comparison packet where agreement, disagreement, and provenance remain co-visible instead of being flattened into two side-by-side answer cards.
watchFor: The shared claim should stay readable while agreement and disagreement evidence settle into separate local basins.
Low-confidence divergence nodes should feel warmer and looser than corroborated evidence.
Citations and figures should stay attached to the evidence they justify.

guideSteps.label: Shared question
guideSteps.description: The comparison revolves around a single question, not two detached chat bubbles.

guideSteps.label: Agreement basin
guideSteps.description: This node anchors the region where both models converge.

guideSteps.label: Divergence basin
guideSteps.description: This low-confidence region captures where the models start to disagree.

guideSteps.label: Trace figure
guideSteps.description: The figure is tied to evidence rather than pinned to a side rail.

tasks.title: Trace the shared support chain
tasks.prompt: Follow the shared claim through consensus evidence, a figure, and a citation.

tasks.title: Find the disagreement pocket
tasks.prompt: Locate the soft divergence region and inspect it without losing the shared question.

tasks.title: Explain the provenance layout
tasks.prompt: Select the provenance node and confirm the runtime still explains why it stayed nearby.

claim.title: Model Comparison
claim.statement: Comparing two model answers is easier when agreement, disagreement, and provenance share one solved surface instead of splitting into isolated panes.

answerBlocks.text: Consensus evidence should cluster without turning into a rigid stack; the reading path remains intact even as focus shifts.
Attribution, citations, and figure traces stay near the answer they justify rather than hiding in a footer.

evidence.text: Both models identify the same evidence cluster and keep it close to the shared question.
The provenance chain stays attached when citations and figures are solved as semantic nodes.
Focus pulls related evidence into a local basin without erasing the opposing region.
Divergent fragments remain close enough to inspect, but distant enough to read as disagreement.
The solver can preserve order and avoid collisions without hardcoding a two-column comparison shell.

contradictions.text: If divergence is flattened into a single score, analysts lose the local structure of disagreement.
Fixed split panes make provenance feel detached from the evidence it belongs to.
If citations drift too far, the comparison surface devolves into annotation hunting.
